target.name: Positron AI
target.founder: Thomas Sohmers, Barrett Woodside, Edward Kmett

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series B), post-money $5B

data.narrative:
Positron AI is a Series B semiconductor company founded by Thomas Sohmers, Barrett Woodside, and Edward Kmett, currently valued at $1.0B+ post-money following $230M+ in total capital raised. The valuation framework draws on a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public semiconductor companies, including stage-adjacent private comparables such as Etched ($5.0B Series B), d-Matrix ($2.0B Series C), and Substrate ($1.0B Series A).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier to the $1.0B last post-money valuation, implying $1.9B. The peer-median method across five same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a $1.0B median valuation, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples is not applicable, as the target's revenue is not disclosed. Public semiconductor comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) trade at a 20.3x P/S median, but cannot be applied without revenue data.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $850M (low) to $2.2B (high), with a median of $1.4B. The current $1.0B+ valuation sits at the lower end of this range. Key uncertainties include the company's actual revenue run rate and capital efficiency relative to peers, which would refine any revenue-based multiple approach.
